2603.19924.title: Translation from the Information Bottleneck Perspective: an Efficiency Analysis of Spatial Prepositions in Bitexts
2603.19924.paper_summary: Efficient communication requires balancing informativity and simplicity when encoding meanings. The Information Bottleneck (IB) framework captures this trade-off formally, predicting that natural language systems cluster near an optimal accuracy-complexity frontier. While supported in visual domains such as colour and motion, linguistic stimuli such as words in sentential context remain unexplored. We address this gap by framing translation as an IB optimisation problem, treating source sentences as stimuli and target sentences as compressed meanings. This allows IB analyses to be performed directly on bitexts rather than controlled naming experiments. We applied this to spatial prepositions across English, German and Serbian translations of a French novel. To estimate informativity, we conducted a pile-sorting pilot-study (N=35) and obtained similarity judgements of pairs of prepositions. We trained a low-rank projection model (D=5) that predicts these judgements (Spearman correlation: 0.78). Attested translations of prepositions lie closer to the IB optimal frontier than counterfactual alternatives, offering preliminary evidence that human translators exhibit communicative efficiency pressure in the spatial domain. More broadly, this work suggests that translation can serve as a window into the cognitive efficiency pressures shaping cross-linguistic semantic systems.
2603.19924.paper_summary_zh: 有效的溝通需要在編碼意義時平衡信息量和簡潔性。信息瓶頸（IB）框架正式捕捉了這一權衡，預測自然語言系統會聚集在最佳準確性-複雜性邊界附近。雖然在顏色和運動等視覺領域得到了支持，但在句子上下文中的詞語等語言刺激仍然未被探索。我們通過將翻譯框架設置為一個IB優化問題來解決這一空白，將源句視為刺激，將目標句視為壓縮的意義。這使得IB分析可以直接在雙語文本上進行，而不是在受控命名實驗中進行。我們將此應用於法語小說的英語、德語和塞爾維亞語翻譯中的空間介詞。為了估計信息量，我們進行了一項堆疊排序的初步研究（N=35），並獲得了介詞對的相似性判斷。我們訓練了一個低秩投影模型（D=5），能夠預測這些判斷（斯皮爾曼相關性：0.78）。已確認的介詞翻譯比反事實替代品更接近IB最佳邊界，提供了初步證據，表明人類翻譯者在空間領域中表現出溝通效率的壓力。更廣泛地說，這項工作表明翻譯可以作為一扇窗口，洞察塑造跨語言語義系統的認知效率壓力。
2603.19924.author: Antoine Taroni et.al.
2603.19924.authors: Antoine Taroni, Ludovic Moncla, Frederique Laforest
